cards.title: Can't Afford a Tutor? Your Child Can Still Thrive
cards.body: Parents often project their own school anxieties onto their children, but your support matters more than expensive tutoring. Your child's potential depends on encouragement, not grammar school entrance fees.

cards.title: Should Kids Climb Neighbour's Fence for Their Ball?
cards.body: One parent worries it teaches trespassing; the other sees harmless childhood play. The debate reveals different parenting philosophies about boundaries and risk.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Weird—Stop the Stereotypes
cards.body: Myths persist about only children being selfish or lonely, yet parents raising single children are increasingly common. These outdated prejudices ignore the reality of modern families.

cards.title: AI Co-Parenting: Are Moms Outsourcing Childcare?
cards.body: Some mothers now use AI tools to manage parenting decisions, reflecting ongoing gender gaps in caregiving. The trend raises questions about technology's role in family life.

cards.title: Should Babies Have Their Own Pension?
cards.body: Parents face pressure to open self-invested personal pensions for young children, adding guilt to already overwhelming parenting duties. The financial advice ignores that many families struggle with their own retirement savings.

cards.title: Intergenerational Trauma and Family Healing
cards.body: Family experiences, including trauma, ripple through generations and affect parenting patterns and child development. Understanding this connection helps parents break harmful cycles.

cards.title: Screen Time Isn't All Bad—Quality Matters Most
cards.body: Interactive screens that engage children can teach critical thinking, while passive viewing may hinder development. Parents should evaluate what their children watch and how they interact with it.

cards.title: Hoarding Risk: Nature vs. Nurture in Families
cards.body: Twin studies show genes account for about 50% of hoarding risk in women, while family environment contributes surprisingly little. Individual life experiences shape the rest.

cards.title: When Helping Your Adult Child Backfires
cards.body: Excessive parental support of adult children can increase dependence and reduce their opportunities for growth. Parents must recognize when helping becomes harmful.

cards.title: Fathers' Music Can Shape Children's Emotional Growth
cards.body: A father's use of music and movement creates steady presence that helps children find their own rhythms. This consistent emotional connection builds family bonds.

cards.title: Mental Health Screening Could Prevent School Crises
cards.body: Routine mental health screenings identify struggling students before distress escalates into crisis situations. Proactive identification is key to safer schools.

cards.title: Building Discipline Starts With Understanding Childhood
cards.body: Self-discipline struggles often trace back to what children didn't receive growing up from their own parents. Understanding this connection helps break cycles and build healthier habits.
